plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name risk.
Buy CRM at 5% as a top-rated quality long setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation and reduced overall risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month return, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk. Expected drawdowns are lower, but returns will also be constrained.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove all below-stop leveraged ETFs.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for reduction.
Buy CRM at 8% as a strong risk/reward long setup.
Buy META at 7% to add another high-scoring long.
This plan balances growth with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month growth, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. It aims for moderate returns with manageable drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all below-stop positions.
Trim QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for reduction.
Buy CRM at 10% to concentrate in a top long setup.
Buy META at 10% to concentrate in another high-scoring long.
Buy NVDU at 5% to add new leveraged ETF exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month stretch growth, leaning into leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher risk and potential for large drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, requiring strict stop discipline to manage losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% for reduced concentration and stability.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETFs.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan aims to park cash for yield and minimize growth-oriented risk.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces growth-oriented risk and high-beta exposure. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account.
